title: Scheduling, Services, And Time Sync
description: Practice RHCSA v9 time synchronization, service control, cron, at, and persistent service enablement.

tasks: Configure clientvm to synchronize time with servervm using chrony and confirm the service is enabled.
Enable and start schedule-note.service so it writes /var/tmp/schedule-note.txt.
Enable and start httpd and make /var/www/html/schedule/index.html available locally.
Create a recurring cron job for root that appends the date to /var/log/rhcsa-cron.log every hour.
Create a one-time at job that writes RHCSA to /root/at-job.txt within the next few minutes.
hints: chronyd should point only at servervm for this lab.
schedule-note.service is already present but disabled.
Use crontab or /etc/cron.d for the recurring job.
Use atq to confirm the at job was accepted.
solution_outline: Configure chrony to use servervm and enable the service.
Enable and start schedule-note.service and httpd, then verify the provided schedule page is available.
Use cron for the recurring task and at for the one-time task.

tasks: Configure chrony to synchronize against servervm and enable the service.
Enable and start schedule-note.service so it writes /var/tmp/schedule-note.txt.
Enable and start httpd and make /var/www/html/schedule/index.html available locally.
Schedule a root cron job to append the date hourly to /var/log/rhcsa-cron.log.
Schedule a one-time at job that writes RHCSA to /root/at-job.txt.
